objections.claim: Section 3 exempts plan-version retirement from the required `SUPERSEDED` record and `supersedes` edge, while frozen `CHANGE-PROTOCOL.md` explicitly requires all three surfaces when retiring an artifact or plan. The repository evidence that plan versions cannot currently be represented as weave nodes exposes a genuine frozen-spec/schema incompatibility; it does not authorize a peer-model applicability exemption. The unanchored “Eye/coordinator” characterization is not an Eye ruling followed by a Daedalus delta, new plan version, TELOS authorization, and renewed implementation authority.

The proposed READ-ONLY oracle is not discriminating. Scanning only `ingest.mjs`, `verify.mjs`, and `detect.mjs` for named fs-write APIs permits writes through local imported modules, aliases or computed properties, `FileHandle` methods, dynamic imports, `child_process`, or other unscanned code. Such an implementation could pass while mutating the repository, so the oracle cannot support the stated machine-enforced READ-ONLY claim.

Candidate discovery is not closed or authority-anchored. “The committed record set,” “the weave snapshot,” and “reusing Lachesis's snapshot loader design” specify neither exact roots/manifest nor a snapshot path, schema, and content identity. A verifier can omit a `SUPERSEDED` record or `supersedes` edge and still return `consistent`, making the golden incapable of proving repository-wide supersession consistency.

Kind detection requires extracting the retired identity from each `supersedes` edge, but edge direction is explicitly deferred. Since both edge endpoints are normally weave-node ids, membership alone cannot determine which endpoint is retired. The proposed detector therefore has no deterministic mechanism for classifying an edge as described.
